6.reason: [The return value of gets() is a char*, so comparing it to the string literal 'NULL' (which is actually a multi-character constant, not the null pointer NULL) is incorrect. It should compare to NULL (without quotes). Also, gets() is unsafe and deprecated, but the main error here is using 'NULL' instead of NULL.]

10.reason: [This condition accesses a[i-1] when i could be 0, leading to out-of-bounds access (undefined behavior). Also, it doesn't check if a[i+1] is within bounds (i.e., not '\0').]
